versions.version_description: Complete and well-aligned chords / intermediate version with split chords and suggestions for playable voicings.
This is the easier version of the original song and it sounds really good..

Inversions used to support melody. The bass track disguises the chords underneath at times. The two solos are quite distinct and in different keys, the former played over the verse, and the latter being a half step up to Gb. Outro solo cycles over those four chords repeated to fade.

tuning: E A D G B E

recommended.song_name: Youve Got To Hide Your Love Away
Picassos Last Words Drink To Me

content:
[Verse 1]
[tab]                       [ch]Bb/F[/ch] [ch]C[/ch]            [ch]Dm[/ch]       [ch]Dm/C[/ch]
I can't wait another day      until I call you[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bb[/ch]      [ch]C[/ch]            [ch]F[/ch]         [ch]A7[/ch]        [ch]Dm[/ch]   [ch]Dm/C[/ch]    [ch]Bb[/ch]        [ch]C/Bb[/ch]
 You've only got my heart on a string and everything aflutter[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]Bb/F[/ch]  [ch]C[/ch]                  [ch]Dm[/ch]    [ch]Dm/C[/ch]
 But another lonely night       might take forever[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bb[/ch]     [ch]C[/ch]             [ch]F[/ch]         [ch]A7[/ch]

[tab]     [ch]Dm[/ch]      [ch]Dm/C[/ch]     [ch]Bb[/ch]
It's all the same to me love[/tab]
[tab]         [ch]C/Bb[/ch]        [ch]F/A[/ch]        [ch]Bb[/ch]
'cause I know what I feel to be right[/tab]


[Chorus]
[tab][ch]Bb[/ch]  [ch]F/Bb[/ch] [ch]Bb[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]  [ch]F[/ch]      [ch]F/C[/ch] [ch]F/A[/ch]  [ch]F[/ch]

[tab][ch]Bb[/ch]      [ch]F/Bb[/ch] [ch]Bb[/ch]  [ch]C[/ch]   [ch]Dm[/ch]
 You're my  guiding light[/tab]
[tab][ch]Gm[/ch]               [ch]Bb/C[/ch]          [ch]Gm/C[/ch]
Day or night I'm always there[/tab]


[Verse 2]
[tab]                      [ch]Bb/F[/ch] [ch]C[/ch]         [ch]Dm[/ch]       [ch]Dm/C[/ch]
May I never miss the thrill of being near you[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bb[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]            [ch]F[/ch]         [ch]A7[/ch]        [ch]Dm[/ch]       [ch]Dm/C[/ch]     [ch]Bb[/ch]      [ch]C/Bb[/ch]
And if it takes a couple of years to turn your tears to laughter[/tab]
